notes: Original Jeopardy-style topoff pack to bring the active bank back to 1000 questions.
Counts are matched to the 2026-05-31 live category deficits after the quality cleanup.

questions.clue: This English author created Hercule Poirot and Miss Marple.
This French author wrote Les Miserables and The Hunchback of Notre-Dame.
This Russian author wrote War and Peace and Anna Karenina.
This Colombian author wrote One Hundred Years of Solitude.
This Italian poet guides readers through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise in the Divine Comedy.
This Dickens novel begins with the line about the best of times and the worst of times.
This Bronte novel features the brooding Mr. Rochester and Thornfield Hall.
This Melville novel sends Ishmael aboard the Pequod in pursuit of a white whale.
This Eliot novel centers on Dorothea Brooke and the town of its title.
This Shakespeare tragedy features the balcony scene and feuding families in Verona.
This Shakespeare comedy sends Viola disguised as Cesario into Illyria.
This Arthur Miller play follows Willy Loman near the end of his salesman life.
This American poet wrote Because I could not stop for Death.
This Greek epic poem tells of Odysseus returning home to Ithaca.
This poet wrote The Waste Land and The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock.
This Roald Dahl book sends a poor boy into Willy Wonka's factory.
This C. S. Lewis novel begins with children entering Narnia through a wardrobe.
questions.answer: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
